      "title": "A Deep-Dive into AAPL Amid the January 2026 Tech Sell-off",
      "url": "https://markets.financialcontent.com/stocks/article/finterra-2026-1-14-apple-under-pressure-a-deep-dive-into-aapl-amid-the-january-2026-tech-sell-off",
      "content": "## Stock Performance Overview

Apple’s stock has historically been a pillar of stability, but the January 2026 sell-off has tested even the most diamond-handed investors.

 1-Year Performance: AAPL saw a meteoric 35% rise in 2025, driven by the "iPhone 17 Supercycle" and the rollout of Apple Intelligence. However, it has shed roughly 8% in the first two weeks of 2026.
 5-Year Performance: Since 2021, the stock has nearly tripled, significantly outperforming the S&P 500.
 10-Year Performance: Long-term holders have seen gains in excess of 800%, adjusted for splits and dividends.

The current sell-off is viewed by analysts not as a structural failure, but as "valuation exhaustion" following the $4 trillion peak.

## Financial Performance [...] Median Price Target: $300.00.
 Bull Case (Wedbush): $350.00, citing the massive "installed base upgrade cycle."
 Bear Case: $215.00, citing regulatory risks and China headwinds.  
   Institutional ownership remains high, as AAPL is still viewed as a "safe-haven" asset during broader market volatility due to its peerless balance sheet.

## Regulatory, Policy, and Geopolitical Factors

Apple is under intense scrutiny from the European Union’s Digital Markets Act (DMA). In 2025, Apple was forced to make iOS interoperable with rival smartwatches and opened its NFC chip to third-party payment providers. Geopolitically, Apple continues to diversify its manufacturing away from China, with India now accounting for nearly 20% of iPhone production—a trend expected to accelerate through 2026. [...] 1. iPhone: The core revenue driver, now entering the "AI Era" with the iPhone 17.
2. Services: Including the App Store, Apple Pay, iCloud+, Apple TV+, and the new "Apple Intelligence Pro" tier. This segment now accounts for over 26% of total revenue.
3. Wearables, Home, and Accessories: Featuring the Apple Watch, AirPods, and the Vision Pro.
4. Mac: High-end computing powered by proprietary M-series silicon.
5. iPad: Tablet dominance across consumer and professional tiers.

The strategy relies on "vertical integration"—controlling everything from the silicon (M4 and A19 chips) to the software (iOS 26) and the retail experience. This allows Apple to command industry-leading gross margins and maintain a customer retention rate that exceeds 90% in most developed markets.",

# Buy the Dip in Apple Stock Before January 29, According to Goldman Sachs

Anushka Dutta

5 min read

In this article:  

AAPL

-0.12%

Tech behemoth Apple (AAPL) is experiencing a dip in its stock performance ahead of first-quarter results for fiscal 2026, which are set to be reported on Jan. 29. AAPL stock is down by 9% over the past month. However, Goldman Sachs analysts see this as an opportune moment to load up on Apple shares. [...] CFO Kevan Parekh revealed that, driven by high consumer satisfaction, the company’s installed base of active devices reached an all-time high across all product categories and geographic segments. Apple’s Q4 EPS increased by 91% from the year-ago value to $1.85, beating the $1.73 per share analyst estimate.

As already stated, Apple is geared to report its Q1 fiscal 2026 results on Jan. 29, after the market closes. Ahead of the results, analysts are bullish about the company’s EPS growth. They expect EPS to climb 10% YOY to $2.65 for Q1. For fiscal 2026, EPS is projected to surge 9% annually to $8.15, followed by 12% growth to $9.10 in the next fiscal year.

## What Do Analysts Think About Apple Stock? [...] Citi also shares this view and expect sApple’s iPhone business to carry the company through. While analysts maintained a bullish “Buy” rating on Apple stock, they also cut the price target from $330 to $315 because they expect a 50% increase in the company’s DRAM costs this year.

Wedbush analyst Dan Ives thinks 2026 could be a “monumental year” for Apple, reiterating an “Outperform” and a Street-high $350 price target. Ives believes that we will see the company “dive into the deep end of the pool on its AI strategic roadmap” this year.",
      "score": 0.79732054
